Provider/utility meter with 7 counter resets (~bimonthly read cycle). All segments stitched via rolling_sum. Boundary artifacts visible in daily chart on reset days — consumption on those days is partially lost.
Provider/utility meter with 3 counter resets (~quarterly). All segments stitched via rolling_sum.
Counter drops and stays dead from Feb 12. Leaf meter — cannot patch.
Counter at zero for entire observation window. Sub-feeder of B616.T33 serving B835.
B665.T42-2-1 reads ~5 MWh/month in Snowflake but Excel STRUX_data shows 0. B664 shows excel=0 vs topology=4.9 MWh. B665 overcredited by ~5 MWh/month. Stale STRUX_data.
Sub-feeder subtracted from B659 but not attributed to any building. ~8 MWh/month unaccounted.

Resolved 2026-04-27: B611.T4-A3/C1/C4 ARE in PME after all, under DB-key aliases B611.T1-4-11/12/13 (display names match the cable-position IDs per DATALOG_ENRICHED). Crosswalk was using the display names as snowflake_id, which didn't resolve. Fixed crosswalk to point at the DB keys; B611/B613/B631 now match Excel within 0.7%.

B665.T42-2-1 has live Snowflake readings (~5 MWh/month) attributed to B664 per Excel formula, but Excel cache for B664 = 0 — STRUX_data value is stale/zero while Snowflake reads real consumption. Same pattern as B616 steam.

H23-1 and H3-1 show inverse May-June deviation from the steady ~70/30 ratio (H3-1 peaks +40%, H23-1 dips ~15%). Total intake rises, so not cross-wiring — consistent with H3-1 carrying summer-only cooling/ventilation loads.

Suspected Excel bug in gtn.xlsx / formula_document.xlsx: B612 row 12 subtracts B652.T8-A3-A14-112 at col AA, in addition to already subtracting its parent B641.T8-A3 at col Z. This double-counts the deduction (~21 MWh/month, B612 understated ~4% in Excel). The ontology previously mirrored this via an extra hasSubMeter edge B612.T8 → B652.T8-A3-A14-112; that edge was removed 2026-04-21 so the ontology is physically honest (single parent B641.T8-A3) and the Excel gap is exposed in reconciliation as +21 MWh/month at B612. Location to inspect/fix: gtn.xlsx, B612 row 12, delete col AA term.

GTN EL reconciliation rows marked 'match' may show small drifts at the building level due to Excel cached aggregation vs PME daily-sum rounding. Classifier uses 0.25% relative threshold (raised from 0.1% on 2026-04-27 to absorb noise-level drifts that aren't real discrepancies).
Resolved 2026-04-27: B611.T4-C1 (Excel allocation lands at B613) was thought to be STRUX-only but is in PME under DB-key alias B611.T1-4-12. Crosswalk fix → B613 matches Excel to ±0.7%.

Resolved 2026-04-27: B611.T4-A3 / B611.T4-C4 (Excel allocations land at B631) were thought to be STRUX-only but are in PME under DB-key aliases B611.T1-4-11 / B611.T1-4-13. Crosswalk fix → B631 matches Excel to ±0.7%.

B652.T14-B3-J reads at building B602 per building_id attribution, but Excel formula subtracts it from B652 (row 33 col T) and adds it to B602. Net campus total is unaffected; per-building attribution differs. Open: decide whether to re-attribute the meter to match Excel or add a topology_override.
Resolved 2026-04-27: 0.2% drift was below the new 0.25% noise floor. Now auto-classifies as 'match'. PME daily-sum vs Excel cached aggregation drift, not a real discrepancy.

GTN EL building formulas cross-checked against reference formula doc. All formulas match except: extra +B616.T31-6-2 term removed from B616 pool. Doc attributes B616.T33-6-1/T33-6-2 to B850, but Excel cache records them under B835 — Excel is canonical, ontology retains B835 attribution (gtn_el_b835_vs_b850 annotation documents the divergence).

Reference formula doc lists B850 = +B616.T33-6-1 +B616.T33-6-2 (implying B850 as the owner building). Excel cache keeps the accounting under B835 (~3089/3185 kWh Jan/Feb); B850 Excel = 0. Ontology retains the Excel-canonical B835 attribution. Rename to B850 was attempted and reverted — doc reflects intent, Excel is facit.

Reference formula doc uses B665.T42_2_03 for B664 and B665 formulas. That ID does not exist in Snowflake; B665.T42-2-1 does. Ontology retains the Snowflake-backed B665.T42-2-1. Suspected doc typo; needs upstream verification.

B616.T33-6-1 flat at zero across the full reference period. Existing B616_T31-6-2_offline annotation covers a related sibling meter. B616's T33 branch feeds B850/B835 (doc-vs-Excel naming divergence already annotated). Inactive sub-meter; accepted.

~8.7% of campus EL intake (averaged over 2025-01 to 2026-02) is not captured by any building-level meter. Lifetime: B660.EL_INTAKE = 98646 MWh, sum of buildings = 90041 MWh, residual = 8605 MWh (8.7%). Seasonal pattern: 3-5% in winter, climbing to 11-14% at the July peak. Likely composition: cable/transformer losses (~3-5% of throughput), B660 substation ancillaries (small), outdoor/perimeter lighting fed direct from MV bus, and a couple of buildings present in PME but not in Excel allocations (B648 ~177 MWh/mo, B669 ~22 MWh/mo) accounting for ~30% of the residual. Visible as the gap between the two lines in the Campus conservation chart.
